title: Gilat to buy Comtech satcoms business six years after failed merger
summary: Gilat Satellite Networks plans to acquire most of Comtech's space-related communications business to expand its defense capabilities. This comes six years after a failed merger attempt between the two companies.

title: Space Force orders two more GPS satellites from Lockheed Martin for $514 million
summary: The U.S. Space Force has ordered two additional GPS satellites from Lockheed Martin for $514 million, bringing the total GPS 3F orders to 14 satellites. These satellites will feature anti-jam capabilities, digital payloads, and upgraded civilian navigation signals.

title: DARPA to explore ways to rapidly rebuild satellite networks if attacked
summary: DARPA is seeking innovative solutions to quickly restore satellite networks in the event of an attack, aiming to resume critical services within hours or weeks. The initiative focuses on developing novel concepts for rapid network recovery.

title: What’s the Best Way to Monetize Space Energy?
summary: The article discusses monetizing space-based energy, highlighting how space infrastructure could become valuable for energy production. Orbital energy solutions are being reconsidered as a viable economic opportunity.

title: Sustained maneuver has a propulsion problem
summary: A spacecraft's sustained maneuver encountered a propulsion problem, highlighting the evolving challenges in space architecture beyond just positioning. The incident underscores the increasing complexity of modern space operations.

title: Missile production push runs into solid rocket motor bottleneck
summary: A CSIS report highlights a bottleneck in solid rocket motor production that could impact planned interceptor purchases by 2027. The supply chain is still recovering from years of consolidation.

title: Revised Artemis lunar lander plans take shape
summary: NASA has outlined updated plans for the Artemis lunar landers, with Blue Origin and SpaceX refining their approaches to accelerate development. The revisions aim to meet the program's goals more efficiently.

title: SpaceX shares rise nearly 20% in historic IPO
summary: SpaceX shares surged nearly 20% on their first day of trading following the company's historic IPO, marking a significant milestone for both SpaceX and the space industry.

title: Avanti trims GEO exposure with Hylas-3 sale
summary: Avanti Communications is selling its Hylas-3 satellite to reduce its geostationary orbit (GEO) exposure. This move marks a shift away from its previous debt-fueled expansion strategy.

title: Astronomers fear orbital data centers will interfere with observations
summary: Astronomers are concerned that SpaceX's planned orbital data center satellites, set to launch as early as next year, could disrupt astronomical observations. The initiative raises fears of increased interference with scientific research.

title: Astrobotic unveils Griffin-1 lunar lander for NASA Moon Base mission
summary: Astrobotic unveiled its Griffin-1 lunar lander, designed to carry one of the heaviest payloads to the Moon for NASA's Moon Base mission.

title: SpaceX sends 24 Starlink satellites to orbit on 1st launch as a public company (video)
summary: SpaceX launched 24 Starlink satellites into orbit using a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base. This was SpaceX's first launch as a public company.

title: 'Everybody came with their A-game': Oscar-winning sound editor Andy Nelson talks reuniting with Spielberg & John Williams for 'Disclosure Day'
summary: Oscar-winning sound editor Andy Nelson discusses the high level of preparation and collaboration during the making of 'Disclosure Day' with Steven Spielberg and John Williams. The project demanded top-tier performance from all involved.

title: NASA X-ray spacecraft discovers supernova wreckage at the heart of the Milky Way
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray spacecraft discovered the supernova wreckage of a dead star that exploded 1,700 years ago, ejecting debris at 2 million miles per hour. The remnants are located at the heart of the Milky Way.

title: Live from the moon! How lasers connected us all to NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts on their epic lunar trip
summary: A laser communication system, tested on the ISS, enabled live video streaming from NASA's Artemis 2 mission during its lunar flyby. This technology marks a significant advancement in space communication.

title: Hubble spies a swarm of stars in a strange, irregular galaxy | Space photo of the day for June 15, 2026
summary: The Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a swarm of stars in an irregular galaxy. The galaxy's faint and unusual structure makes it a compelling subject for observation.

title: Scientists find strange changes on sun hours before a powerful X9 solar flare: 'I was not expecting what I found'
summary: Scientists observed unusual changes on the Sun just hours before a powerful X9 solar flare, which could improve space weather forecasting. The unexpected findings highlight the need for better monitoring of solar activity.

title: Our experts recommend these image-stabilized Canon binoculars for stunning, steady views of the night sky, now $100 off in this early anti-Prime Day deal
summary: Canon's image-stabilized binoculars are recommended for high-magnification stargazing and are currently discounted in an early anti-Prime Day deal. The binoculars are praised for providing shake-free views of celestial events like the June moon and Mercury conjunction.

title: We have 4 fundamental forces of nature. 'Quantum gravity' could help lead us to a mysterious 5th
summary: Scientists propose that a new framework for quantum gravity may provide insights into a potential fifth fundamental force of nature. This could revolutionize our understanding of the universe's fundamental laws.

title: Explore JPL to Take Place Oct. 10, 11
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) is hosting an open-house event called Explore JPL on October 10 and 11 to celebrate its 90th anniversary. Visitors can tour iconic facilities and explore thematic areas like Moon to Mars and Missions That Changed the World.

title: NASA’s Chandra Finds Unexpected Fireworks in Aftermath of Stellar Explosions
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory discovered unexpected changes in brightness among supernova remnants in the galaxy Messier 83. This challenges the usual observation of slowly fading remnants after stellar explosions.

title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from New Jersey Students
summary: NASA astronauts Chris Williams and Jessica Meir will answer STEM questions from New Jersey students during a live Earth-to-space call from the International Space Station. The event will be streamed on NASA's YouTube channel on June 18.

title: NASA’s SpaceX CRS-34 Dragon Returns Packed with Space Station Science
summary: NASA's SpaceX CRS-34 Dragon spacecraft returned to Earth with a significant payload of scientific samples and hardware from the International Space Station. The mission marked the 34th SpaceX commercial resupply mission for NASA.

title: Frontiers Forum Speaker Series
summary: NASA is hosting a public speaker series featuring experts discussing agency missions and aerospace technology. The series will cover topics like the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon Available for Prelaunch Virtual Interviews
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon will be available for virtual prelaunch interviews on June 22 to discuss his upcoming mission to the International Space Station as part of Expeditions 74/75. The interviews will be conducted from the Gagarin Cosmonaut Training Center in Star City, Russia.

title: San Francisco’s Patchwork Streets
summary: An astronaut aboard the International Space Station captured a photograph of downtown San Francisco and nearby areas on May 27, 2026, showcasing the Golden Gate Bridge and the San Francisco-Oakland Bay Bridge.

title: Experience the Launch of NASA’s Roman Space Telescope
summary: NASA invites digital creators and social media users to register for a NASA Social event to experience the launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which aims to reveal distant worlds, dark energy, and cosmic structure.

title: Metrics
summary: NASA has released the FY26 Services Catalog and Service Level Agreement (SLA) for its NSSC services, detailing service descriptions, rates, and performance indicators.

title: Pumice Rafts Encroach on Admiralty Islands
summary: Buoyant volcanic pumice rafts from an underwater eruption drifted into the Bismarck Sea, choking the coasts of the Admiralty Islands. The phenomenon highlights the unexpected impacts of volcanic activity on marine ecosystems.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace, a German private space launch provider, canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The company, based in Ottobrunn, had been preparing for the launch for weeks.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. Flight 12 was completed about three weeks prior to these developments.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its first launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit. This marks a key step toward the agency's planned crewed lunar landing.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: Three Starlink launches, two Rocket Lab missions, and a Chinese launch are scheduled for the upcoming week from multiple locations including China, California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars, which lasted six months longer than planned. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon on Mars.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its Blue Moon and Mars missions despite the recent failure of the New Glenn rocket during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovery and future objectives following the anomaly.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of European space industry milestones.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its main instrument, ASPIICS, are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: The ESA's Prodex programme enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway, supporting scientific research in space. Prodex helps research institutes participate in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and validating materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative contributions to safeguard spacecraft assembly.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance accessibility and efficiency in space-related activities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory conducted a 'bake-out' test on the Mars 2020 parachute to remove moisture and ensure its performance during the Perseverance rover's landing. This test is part of preparations for the upcoming Mars mission.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to avoid microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will help safely land the rover in 2028 to search for signs of life.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: Space stocks declined following SpaceX's successful IPO, as investors pulled back from other publicly traded space companies. The dip affected major players across the sector.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six payloads to space from Japan's Tanegashima Space Center.

title: VCs Predict the SpaceX IPO Will Lift the Entire Industry
summary: Venture capitalists anticipate that SpaceX's potential IPO will positively impact the broader space industry by boosting investor confidence and attracting more capital. The move could shift the industry's reliance on private funding toward public markets.

title: Op-ed: Up to a Trillion Dollars is About to Chase Space. What Kind of Economy Will Be Built?
summary: A trillion dollars is expected to flow into the space industry, raising questions about the type of economy that will emerge from this capital influx. The largest IPO in history is poised to drive record investment into space ventures.

title: What the SpaceX IPO Means for Europe’s Space Industry
summary: SpaceX's IPO in New York is a significant event for the global space industry, with major implications for Europe's space sector. The move could reshape competition and investment dynamics in the industry.

title: Exclusive: Tilebox Launches AI-Focused Update
summary: Tilebox launched an AI-focused update to enhance the effectiveness of AI agents as geospatial-data analysts. The update aims to improve data processing and analysis capabilities in the space industry.

title: Analysts Debate SpaceX’s $1.75T Valuation Ahead of IPO
summary: Analysts are divided on SpaceX's $1.75 trillion valuation ahead of its IPO, with debates over its accuracy and short-term stock performance. However, there is consensus that the space industry is experiencing significant growth.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC satellite, marking its entry into the orbital data center market. The company aims to leverage this technology for advanced space-based data processing.

title: Orbital Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Build ODC Constellation
summary: Orbital secures $5 million in pre-seed funding to develop its ODC constellation, which uses smaller satellites to handle large AI inference workloads and maintain constant ground contact.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to deploy at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing conditions to expedite launches. The decision aims to balance regulatory flexibility with the need for timely deployment.

title: Anuvu Names Former HBO Exec as CEO
summary: Anuvu appointed former HBO executive Simon Sutton as its new CEO, replacing longtime leader Josh Marks. The announcement was made on June 15.

title: Gilat to Acquire Comtech’s Satellite and Space Business, Targeting US Defense Growth
summary: Gilat Satellite Networks announced the acquisition of Comtech’s Satellite and Space Communications segment to strengthen its position in the U.S. defense market. The deal combines two major players in satellite ground technology.

title: Israel’s El Al Airline to Adopt Starlink for IFC
summary: Israel's El Al Airline will adopt SpaceX's Starlink for in-flight connectivity, with service rolling out gradually. The agreement marks a significant step for Starlink's expansion in the aviation sector.

title: SpaceX Begins Trading at $150 Making Elon Musk the World’s First Trillionaire on Paper
summary: SpaceX started public trading on Nasdaq at $150 per share, an 11% increase over its IPO price, valuing the company enough to make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire on paper.

title: Satellite IoT is Becoming Essential Infrastructure as NTN and Cellular IoT Services Converge
summary: Satellite IoT is evolving into essential infrastructure as Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) and cellular IoT services converge, addressing the growing needs of enterprises managing remote assets. The integration highlights the critical role of satellite connectivity in modern operations.

title: U.S. State Department Taps SpaceX’s Starlink for Two-Year Disaster Response Initiative
summary: The U.S. Department of State signed a two-year agreement with SpaceX to use Starlink for improved disaster response and humanitarian aid. This initiative aims to enhance global disaster preparedness using SpaceX's satellite internet technology.

title: Canadian Government Awards EO Satellite Contracts to Calian, Kepler, MDA Space
summary: The Canadian government awarded contracts worth C$2.4 million to Calian, Kepler, and MDA Space to enhance Earth Observation (EO) satellite capabilities. This move aims to strengthen Canada's EO infrastructure.

title: Viasat Secures PTS-G U.S. Space Force SSC Swarm 1 Contract
summary: Viasat has secured a contract from the U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command to build, launch, and deliver the first satellite in a proliferated fleet for the Swarm 1 program. This contract marks a significant step in Viasat's involvement in U.S. military space initiatives.

title: SpaceX Set for IPO, Banking on Faith in Elon Musk and Massive AI Growth
summary: SpaceX is preparing for its IPO on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SPCX, following 24 years as a privately held company. The move is driven by investor confidence in Elon Musk and expectations of significant AI growth.

title: Applied Atomics Emerges from Stealth with Enough Backing to Build Star Reacher Network
summary: Applied Atomics has come out of stealth mode with over $500 million in demand commitments to develop the Star Reacher Network, a mobility infrastructure layer for space. The company aims to build a network to support space transportation and logistics.
